title: Publish release after completed serial recalibration WIs
outOfScope: Changing the release workflow implementation or enterprise-security boundary.
Publishing or asserting provider assets before the release workflow produces them.

sources.reason: Advanced canonical candidate state and source identity.
Release preparation and publication workflow documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: Release preparation is fail-closed and provider publication remains an explicit external verification step.

executionDecision.reason: The user-authorized serial plan permits this release Work Item after the completed preceding WIs; Work Item 1 remains explicitly not started.

preReviewWarnings: Verify exact source identity, candidate/public projection separation, and no enterprise-security overclaim.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: release.json promotes v0.5.37 and next-release.json remains a distinct v0.5.38 candidate.
release-state.json records candidate_prepared v0.5.38 with the latest main source identity and valid metadata digests.
The release workflow binds detached source, tag, provider asset, Workflow Run, SBOM, provenance, digest, smoke, and compatibility evidence.
Release preparation checks pass without asserting unavailable provider assets or changing enterprise-security NO-GO conclusions.
All declared AI and project quality checks pass.
guidelines: Release claims must be bound to independently verifiable source and artifact evidence.
Do not claim provider publication or enterprise security beyond available evidence.

intent.problem: The completed serial remediation requires a new release preparation snapshot whose public and candidate metadata, source identity, and evidence baselines remain internally consistent before provider publication.
constraints: Use v0.5.37 as the next public tag and keep v0.5.38 as the unpublished candidate.
The release workflow must resolve the post-merge default-branch commit and independently generate provider assets.
Do not start Work Item 1 or claim provider assets before the workflow produces them.
intent.rationale: A release PR must prepare exact metadata and evidence boundaries while leaving immutable tag and provider publication to the release workflow after merge.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Candidate/public metadata are distinct and canonical state projections agree.
Release preparation accepts the next patch while public tag publication remains pending.
Evidence and enterprise boundary remain bounded until provider workflow evidence exists.

rawUserRequest: Execute the current recalibration plan serially through complete Work Item lifecycles, keep Work Item 1 unstarted, and proceed to the authorized release Work Item after documentation alignment.

declaredIntent.summary: Prepare and publish the next release with exact source-bound evidence and no unsupported enterprise-security claims.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized continuation through the release Work Item in the task conversation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
